title: State Street to Participate in Autonomous Bernstein’s 39th Annual Strategic Decisions Conference
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) announced today that its Chairman & CEO, Ron O’Hanley, and Vice Chairman & Chief Financial Officer, Eric Aboaf, will participate in Autonomous Bernstein’s 39th Annual Strategic Decisions Conference in New York on Wednesday, May 31, 2023 at 9:00 am ET. An audio webcast will be accessible on the home page of State Street’s Investor Relations website, investors.statestreet.com. A recorded replay will be available on the Investor Relations website later that day, for approximately ninety days following the presentation. About State Street Corporation State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) is one of the world's leading providers of financial services to institutional investors including investment servicing, investment management and investment research and trading. With $37.6 trillion in assets under custody and/or administration and $3.6 trillion* in assets under management as of March 31, 2023. State Street operates globally in more than 100 geographic markets and employs approximately 43,000 worldwide. For more information, visit State Street's website at www.statestreet.com. *Assets under management as of March 31, 2023 includes approximately $65 billion of assets with respect to SPDR® products for which State Street Global Advisors Funds Distributors, LLC (SSGA FD) acts solely as the marketing agent. SSGA FD and State Street Global Advisors are affiliated.

title: State Street, Schwab See Deposits Drop
text: Banks across the board are under pressure to pay depositors more as interest rates continue to rise.

title: Why State Street Stock Is Sliding After Announcing Earnings
text: State Street (NYSE: STT) shares have declined by more than 10% in today's trading session, past any standard deviations experienced in the past on a normalized basis by investors. This reaction is attributed to market sentiment reflecting what has been reported in the latest quarterly earnings.

title: Factbox: Deposit flight from U.S. regional banks raise questions about funding costs
text: Regional banks in the United States on Monday reported deposit outflows between 3% and 11% from the prior quarter after customers sought the refuge of bigger banks in the aftermath of one of the worst banking crises.

title: Bank earnings ‘off to a good start,' with Bank of America and Goldman Sachs on deck
text: First-quarter earnings from the largest U.S. banks are off to a mostly positive beginning despite a tough start to the year for the sector, analysts at Jefferies said Monday.

title: Why Shares of State Street Are Plunging Today
text: Earnings missed analyst estimates. The bank saw a decline in fee revenue.

title: State Street Corporation (STT) Q1 Earnings and Revenues Lag Estimates
text: State Street Corporation (STT) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.52 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.62 per share. This compares to earnings of $1.59 per share a year ago.

title: State Street (STT) Dips as Q1 Earnings Miss on Higher Costs
text: State Street (STT) records a marginal rise in revenues in the first quarter of 2023.

title: State Street shares sink on 1Q earnings miss
text: State Street shares fell before the opening bell on Monday after the Boston-headquartered financial services company's first quarter results failed to meet expectations on both profit and revenue. The firm posted earnings per share of $1.52 and revenue of $3.101 billion, below the consensus expectations as per FactSet of earnings per share of $1.64 and revenue of $3.126 billion.

title: State Street profit misses on lower fee income; shares fall
text: State Street Corp reported a first-quarter profit on Monday that missed analysts' estimates, hurt by a fall in fee income due to the recent U.S. banking crisis, sending the company's shares down 10% in premarket trading.

title: Charles Schwab Stock Rises After Earnings Beat, Pauses Buybacks; M&T Rallies, State Street Dives
text: Charles Schwab stock rose early Monday after it beat Q1 earnings estimates. M&T Bank, State Street report results following March bank panic.

title: State Street stock drops on earnings miss tied to lower fee revenue
text: State Street Corp. STT, +1.55% stock fell 6% in premarket trades after the Boston-based financial firm missed consensus estimates for profit and revenue. State Street said its first-quarter net income fell to $549 million, or $1.52 a share, from $604 million, or $1.57 a share, in the year-ago quarter.

title: State Street Reports First-Quarter 2023 Financial Results
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) reported its first-quarter 2023 financial results today. The news release, presentation, and additional financial information can be accessed on State Street's Investor Relations website, http://investors.statestreet.com. A conference call to discuss the firm's financial results, outlook and related matters will be held at 9:00 a.m. ET today, Monday, April 17, 2023. The call will be open to the public.

title: Earnings Preview: Netflix, Tesla and More

text: State Street Corporation (STT) has an impressive earnings surprise history and currently possesses the right combination of the two key ingredients for a likely beat in its next quarterly report.

title: Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, BNY Mellon and State Street top Jefferies list for Q1 bank earnings
text: Jefferies analysts on Tuesday said Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs Group Inc., Bank of New York Mellon Corp. and State Street Corp. remain more attractive than other big banks ahead of first-quarter earnings season.
